narrative:
**Where We Stand**
We're tied with everyone else at 0 points to start Pool C play. The tournament is just beginning and every team is on equal footing.

**Our Next Game**
We face the Ennismore Eagles on Friday April 10th at 9:15 AM. Rankings suggest we match up well, but they'll come out hungry and anything can happen in tournament hockey.

**Around the Pool**
At the same 9:15 AM time slot, we'll want to keep an eye on Kincardine playing Windsor. The scenario analysis shows we win the pool in 156 out of 729 possible outcomes, illustrating there are multiple paths forward depending on how results fall. Later at 3:45 PM, we get Windsor while Ennismore takes on top-ranked Kincardine - those results will shape the pool standings heading into the final day.

scouting.description: This season we've faced several teams heading to Provincials across 5 tournaments. We swept St. Catharines Brock Jr. Badgers in two meetings (6-0, 2-0). Against the rest... let's just say we're looking for redemption. Of note: Durham West Lightning (#29) is in our quarterfinal opponent's pool (Pool F) -- they beat us 2-0 in November, but they're not the favourite to win their pool. In tournament play, anything can happen!

coaches_corner.don_cherry: I'll tell ya, these Kanata Rangers girls are gonna shock some people - forget what the numbers say, it's all about heart and hustle when the puck drops! You kids out there watching, this is how girls hockey keeps growing - pure determination and beautiful hockey!
coaches_corner.talking_points:
**Mindset** — This is our tournament opener and a chance to set the tone for everything that follows. Start strong, play our system, and establish our identity right from the opening faceoff.

**The Opponent** — Ennismore earned their spot here just like we did, and they'll be hungry to prove themselves against a higher-ranked team. Expect them to come out fast and physical, looking to catch us off guard early.

**What We Need** — Only pool winners advance, so every shift matters in this game. Stick to our game plan, support each other, and execute the fundamentals we've been working on all season.

**Discipline** — We're starting with a clean slate on penalties, so let's keep our emotions in check and play smart hockey. Make them beat us with skill, not by giving them power play opportunities.
